documentation:
# Symbolic Dalitz-Plot Decomposition

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache_2.0-blue.svg)](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)

[![PyPI package](https://badge.fury.io/py/ampform-dpd.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/ampform-dpd)
[![Supported Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/ampform-dpd)](https://pypi.org/project/ampform-dpd)

[![CI status](https://github.com/ComPWA/ampform-dpd/workflows/CI/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ComPWA/ampform-dpd/actions/workflows/ci.yml?query=branch%3Amain+workflow%3Aci)
[![pre-commit.ci status](https://results.pre-commit.ci/badge/github/ComPWA/ampform-dpd/main.svg)](https://results.pre-commit.ci/latest/github/ComPWA/ampform-dpd/main)
[![Test coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/ComPWA/ampform-dpd/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/ComPWA/ampform-dpd)

[![code style: prettier](https://img.shields.io/badge/code_style-prettier-ff69b4.svg?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/prettier/prettier)
[![Spelling checked](https://img.shields.io/badge/cspell-checked-brightgreen.svg)](https://github.com/streetsidesoftware/cspell/tree/master/packages/cspell)
[![Ruff](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/charliermarsh/ruff/main/assets/badge/v2.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff)
[![uv](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/astral-sh/uv/main/assets/badge/v0.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv)

This repository is a (temporary) extension of [AmpForm](https://ampform.rtfd.io) and provides a symbolic implementation of Dalitz-plot decomposition ([10.1103/PhysRevD.101.034033](https://journals.aps.org/prd/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevD.101.034033)) with [SymPy](https://www.sympy.org/en/index.html). It has been extracted from the [ComPWA/polarimetry](https://github.com/ComPWA/polarimetry) repository ([10.1007/JHEP07(2023)228](<https://doi.org/10.1007/JHEP07(2023)228>)).

Installation instructions can be found [here](https://compwa.github.io/ampform-dpd/#installation).

modules.name: ampform_dpd.io
documentation:
Input-output functions for `ampform` and `sympy` objects.

This module extends AmpForm's {func}`~ampform.io.aslatex` function. You can register
more implementations as follows:

```pycon
>>> from ampform_dpd.io import aslatex
>>> @aslatex.register(int)
... def _(obj: int) -> str:
...     return "my custom rendering"
>>> aslatex(1)
'my custom rendering'
>>> aslatex(3.4 - 2j)
'3.4-2i'
```

This code originates from [ComPWA/ampform#280](https://github.com/ComPWA/ampform/pull/280).

functions.name: ampform_dpd.io.as_markdown_table
documentation: Render objects a `str` suitable for generating a table.

functions.name: ampform_dpd.io._determine_item_type
documentation:
Determine the type of the items in a sequence.

```pycon
>>> _determine_item_type([1, 2, 3])
<class 'int'>
>>> _determine_item_type([True, False])
<class 'bool'>
>>> _determine_item_type([True, False, 1])
<class 'int'>
>>> _determine_item_type([3.14, 1 + 1j])
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
ValueError: Not all items are of type float'
```

functions.name: ampform_dpd.io.perform_cached_lambdify
documentation:
Lambdify a SymPy `~sympy.core.expr.Expr` and cache the result to disk.

The cached result is fetched from disk if the hash of the expression is the same as
the hash embedded in the filename.

Args:
: expr: A `sympy.Expr <sympy.core.expr.Expr>` on which to call

  : {func}`~tensorwaves.function.sympy.create_function` or
    {func}`~tensorwaves.function.sympy.create_parametrized_function`.

  parameters: Specify this argument in order to create a

  : `~tensorwaves.function.ParametrizedBackendFunction` instead of a
    `~tensorwaves.function.PositionalArgumentFunction`.

  backend: The choice of backend for the created numerical function. **WARNING**:

  : this function has only been tested for {code}`backend="jax"`!

  directory: The directory in which to cache the result. If `None`, the cache

  : directory will be put under the home directory, or to the path specified by
    the environment variable {code}`SYMPY_CACHE_DIR`.

:::{seealso}
{func}`ampform.sympy.perform_cached_doit`
:::

functions.name: ampform_dpd.io.simplify_latex_rendering
documentation: Improve LaTeX rendering of an `~sympy.tensor.indexed.Indexed` object.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.io.serialization.format.Topology
documentation: Topology definition as a list of final state IDs.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.io.serialization.format.Node
documentation: Node definition within a `.Topology`.

modules.name: ampform_dpd.io.serialization
documentation:
Amplitude model serialization

See {doc}`/serialization` for more information and
<https://rub-ep1.github.io/amplitude-serialization> for more information.

:::{warning}
This module is in preview, see <https://github.com/ComPWA/ampform-dpd/issues/133> for
updates.
:::

functions.name: ampform_dpd.io.serialization.dynamics.to_mandelstam_symbol
documentation:
Create a Mandelstam symbol for a node.

```pycon
>>> to_mandelstam_symbol([3, 2])
sigma1
>>> to_mandelstam_symbol([1, [2, 3]])
m0
```

functions.name: ampform_dpd.io.serialization.dynamics.to_mass_symbol
documentation:
Create a mass symbol for a node.

```pycon
>>> to_mass_symbol(1)
m1
>>> to_mass_symbol((1, 2))
sigma3
```

functions.name: ampform_dpd.io.serialization.decay.get_spectator_id
documentation:
Get the spectator ID from a reference topology.

```pycon
>>> get_spectator_id([1, [2, 3]])
1
```

modules.name: ampform_dpd
documentation: Module for formulating the amplitude model for a three-body decay using DPD.

methods.name: ampform_dpd.DalitzPlotDecompositionBuilder.__init__
documentation:
Amplitude builder for the helicity formalism with Dalitz-plot decomposition.

Args:

: decay: The `.ThreeBodyDecay` over which to formulate the amplitude model.
  min_ls: Use helicity couplings instead of

  > $LS$-couplings. If setting this boolean with a `tuple`, the first
  > element of the `tuple` defines whether to use helicity couplings on the
  > **production** `.IsobarNode` and the second configures the **decay**
  > `.IsobarNode`.

modules.name: ampform_dpd.decay
documentation: Data structures that describe a three-body decay.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.InitialStateID
documentation: ID for the initial state particle in a three-body decay.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.FinalStateID
documentation: ID for a particle in the final state of a three-body decay.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.StateID
documentation: ID for any of the initial state or final state particles in a three-body decay.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.StateIDTemplate
documentation: Generic template for the ID of a particle in a three-body decay.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.InitialState
documentation: The initial state particle.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.FinalState
documentation: One of the final state particles.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.ParentType
documentation: Type of the parent of an `IsobarNode`.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.ProductionNode
documentation: The first `IsobarNode` in a `ThreeBodyDecayChain`.

variables.name: ampform_dpd.decay.DecayNode
documentation: The second `IsobarNode` in a `ThreeBodyDecayChain`.

classes.name: ampform_dpd.decay.State
documentation: Initial or final state `.Particle` in a `ThreeBodyDecay`, carrying an index.

modules.name: ampform_dpd.version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: ampform_dpd.spin
documentation: Functions for generating spin projections and LS couplings.

functions.name: ampform_dpd.spin.generate_ls_couplings
documentation:
Generate a list of allowed LS couplings.

```pycon
>>> generate_ls_couplings(1.5, 0.5, 0)
[(1, 1/2), (2, 1/2)]
```

functions.name: ampform_dpd.spin.filter_parity_violating_ls
documentation:
Filter parity-violating LS combinations from a list of LS couplings.

```pycon
>>> LS = generate_ls_couplings(0.5, 1.5, 0)  # Λc → Λ(1520)π
>>> LS
[(1, 3/2), (2, 3/2)]
>>> filter_parity_violating_ls(LS, +1, -1, -1)
[(2, 3/2)]
```

functions.name: ampform_dpd.spin.create_spin_range
documentation:
Create a range of allowed spin projections.

```pycon
>>> create_spin_range(1.5)
[-3/2, -1/2, 1/2, 3/2]
```

functions.name: ampform_dpd.spin.create_rational_range
documentation:
Create a range of rational numbers, especially useful for spin projections.

```pycon
>>> create_rational_range(-0.5, +1.5)
[-1/2, 1/2, 3/2]
```

functions.name: ampform_dpd.adapter.qrules.normalize_state_ids
documentation: Relabel the state IDs so that they lie in the range $[0, N)$.

functions.name: ampform_dpd.adapter.qrules._impl_normalize_state_ids
documentation: Relabel the state IDs so that they lie in the range $[0, N)$.

returns.name:
(
    tuple[()]
    | tuple[FinalStateID, FinalStateID]
    | tuple[FinalStateID, FinalStateID, FinalStateID]
)

variables.name: ampform_dpd.adapter.qrules.T
documentation: Type variable for the input and output of {func}`normalize_state_ids`.

modules.name: ampform_dpd.angles
documentation: Formulate expressions for scattering and alignment angles.

functions.name: ampform_dpd.angles.formulate_scattering_angle
documentation:
Formulate the scattering angle in the rest frame of the resonance.

Compute the $\theta_{ij}$ scattering angle as formulated in [Eq (A1) in the
DPD paper](https://journals.aps.org/prd/pdf/10.1103/PhysRevD.101.034033#page=9).
The angle is that between particle $i$ and spectator particle $k$ in the
rest frame of the isobar resonance $(ij)$.

functions.name: ampform_dpd.angles.formulate_theta_hat_angle
documentation: Formulate an expression for $\hat\theta_{i(j)}$.

functions.name: ampform_dpd.angles.formulate_zeta_angle
documentation: Formulate an expression for the alignment angle $\zeta^i_{j(k)}$.

modules.name: ampform_dpd._attrs
documentation: Helper functions for constructing `attrs` decorated classes.

modules.name: ampform_dpd.dynamics.builder
documentation:
Dynamics builder functions for {meth}`.register_builder`.

:::{note}
As opposed to [AmpForm](https://ampform.rtfd.io), AmpForm-DPD defines
dynamics over the **entire decay chain**, not a single isobar node. The dynamics
classes and the corresponding builders would have to be extended to implement other
dynamics lineshapes.
:::

modules.name: ampform_dpd.dynamics
documentation: Functions for dynamics lineshapes and kinematics.
